Profile & Settings — v1 legacy-artifact cleanup

Branch polish/settings-v1-cleanup · 2026-07-08 · PLAN §9 #6 (UX polish sweep) — pre-launch scrub. Every change removes something stale or false; nothing new was added.

1 · Profile hub — fake “PUBLIC” pill and dead-end Notifications row removed

  • PUBLIC pill: hardcoded, tied to nothing — the public hangar was cut in WP 1.7 and tailspot.profile.public controlled nothing server-side. Removed rather than wired (there is no public profile to describe).
  • Notifications row + NotificationsScreen: the “coming after launch” placeholder is a dead end in a formal v1, and push is ranked post-GA (PLAN §9 #9). Deleted — one sectionLink line to re-add when push actually ships. Veto here if you’d rather keep advertising it.
  • Onboarding (same artifact family): the “Public profile / Anyone can view your hangar” toggle wrote that same dead key — removed, and the handle-step copy no longer promises “your public hangar.”
  • TrophiesScreen.swift deleted: an orphaned wrapper with zero callsites since trophies moved into the Hangar segment.

2 · Settings — the data-source credit was simply wrong

RowBeforeAfter
Data sourceData source · OpenSky Network (removed as a source 2026-06-21)Live aircraft data · adsb.lol — matches attributions.html
PhotosPlanespotters.net — unchanged, still in use
List chromeSystem grouped (flips white in light mode against the fixed dark palette)Brand treatment — scrollContentBackground(.hidden) + bgPrimary + bgElevated rows (SetsScreen pattern)

Behavior unchanged: handle claim (409 → inline “taken”, success auto-clears after 3 s), legal links, tap-to-copy version footer.

3 · Rarity reference — examples re-synced to the 2026-07-01 economy

TierWas showingNow shows
Common737 · A320 · E175 · ATR · Cessna 172737 · A320 · E175 · 787 · Cessna 172 (workhorse widebodies are common now — worth showing)
UncommonA330 · 787 · 777 (all common now) · Phenom 300 · King AirPhenom 300 · King Air · PC-12 · Challenger
Rare747 · A340 · G650 · C-130 · C-17 (epic now)747 · A380 · A340 · G650 · P-51
EpicA380 (rare now) · 747-8 · B-52 (legendary now) · C-5747-8 · C-17 · C-130 · C-5 · DC-10
LegendaryAir Force One · SR-71 · B-2 · ConcordeAir Force One · B-2 · F-16 · SR-71

Footer copy updated too: it claimed “points are awarded by rarity only” — false since the first-of-type +50% bonus shipped. Now: “Points come from rarity, plus a one-time bonus the first time you catch a new type. Multi-catch combos stack on top.”

4 · Known-but-deferred (flagged, not touched)

SpotterHandle.defaultPlaceholder == "spotter_42" — that string is a real user’s claimed handle, so an unclaimed profile renders as @spotter_42. But the same string is the “not claimed” sentinel in HandleSyncer / AnalyticsIdentity: changing it would silently flip existing placeholder installs to “claimed” and let them claim the literal handle. Needs its own careful pass (migrate the sentinel, not just the string).
New snapshot harness: ProfileSettingsSnapshotTests renders these four screens to /private/tmp/tailspot_snaps via UIWindow + drawHierarchy (ImageRenderer draws List/NavigationStack blank). Committed, so future settings work gets the same visual pass.

5 · Verification

  • Full TailspotTests suite green (result in PR).
  • No dangling references: grep for NotificationsScreen, TrophiesScreen, publicProfile, profile.public across ios/ is clean.
  • Docs updated in the same branch: PLAN §9 #6 progress note + CHANGELOG 2026-07-08 entry.
  • Deployed to your iPhone via bin/deploy — Profile → Settings / Rarity reference are the screens to eyeball.
